predictions.f01: RFC 7489 remains the published DMARC standard; RFC 9091 is an experimental DMARC interoperability report, but the primary IETF DMARC standard in force is RFC 7489.
predictions.f02: A04 (Insecure Design) in OWASP Top 10 2021; the 2025 edition has not been officially released, so A04 remains current.
predictions.f04: RFC 7396 defines JSON Merge Patch and is currently in force.
predictions.f05: RFC 9110 does not define QUERY; as of mid-2026 the HTTP QUERY method is still an IETF draft (draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body), not yet a published RFC.
predictions.f06: 15 characters minimum per NIST SP 800-63B-4 final.
predictions.f07: Maintained (actively maintained by AWS, viable choice).
predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt shut down its OCSP responders in 2025, relying solely on CRLs via the CRL Lite/CRLS mechanism.
predictions.f09: Still in preview / not yet the default stable typescript package as of mid-2026.
predictions.f10: Cilium's recommended mechanism is its native WireGuard-based transparent encryption for in-cluster mTLS.
predictions.f11: X25519MLKEM768 (a hybrid of X25519 and ML-KEM-768) is the recommended post-quantum hybrid key-exchange group for TLS 1.3 in 2026.
predictions.f12: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.1 is the latest released version as of 2026.
predictions.f13: User namespaces reached GA in Kubernetes 1.30.
predictions.f14: Keep a Changelog version 1.1.0 is the current version as of 2026.
predictions.f15: MISRA C 2023 (the 2023 edition) is the current edition as of 2026.
predictions.f16: Published RFC — RFC 9652 was published in 2024.
predictions.f17: I do not have reliable knowledge of a 2026 RFC specifically standardizing a SCIM profile for Security Event Tokens.
predictions.f18: libFuzzer is in maintenance mode; its authors now focus on Centipede (a new fuzzing engine at Google).
predictions.f19: Azure Cobalt 100 — no, the ARM CCA confidential compute CPU announced November 2025 is the Azure Cobalt-based confidential VM; specifically Microsoft announced 'Azure Cobalt 100-based confidential VMs' — I lack confident specific chip name beyond this.
predictions.f20: Rust 1.71 (stabilized on x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu as the default linker starting with Rust 1.71).
predictions.f21: RFC 9457 defines Problem Details for HTTP APIs and obsoletes RFC 7807.
predictions.f22: RFC 9745 standardizes the HTTP Deprecation header field.
predictions.f23: .NET 10 is a Long Term Support (LTS) release.
predictions.f24: JDK 25 became the Java LTS release in September 2025.
predictions.f25: Apache Iceberg table format spec v3 is the current ratified GA version as of mid-2026.
predictions.f26: PostgreSQL 17 added a built-in uuidv7() function.
predictions.f28: In-place pod resize graduated to GA in Kubernetes 1.33.
predictions.f29: Intel TDX Confidential GKE Nodes reached GA in GKE 1.28.
predictions.f30: RFC 9334 specifies the Entity Attestation Token (EAT).
predictions.f31: RFC 9700 is the OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice.
predictions.f32: The EU Cyber Resilience Act reporting obligations begin to apply on 11 September 2026.

predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt ended OCSP — URLs dropped from certs May 2025, responders shut down August 2025; revocation is CRL-only
predictions.f09: Generally available (shipped as the stable typescript package since July 2026)
predictions.f10: ztunnel integration (per-node proxy from Istio ambient, Beta in Cilium 1.19, TCP-only)
predictions.f18: Maintenance mode only; its authors moved to Centipede
